stories.title: Skubal Speaks — "My Preference Is to Finish the Season as a Tiger"
stories.angle: Tarik Skubal said publicly on July 23 that his "preference is to finish the season with the Tigers and win the World Series." He acknowledged his next start could be his last in Detroit. ESPN's Jeff Passan and Kiley McDaniel put the trade odds at 60%. The Dodgers are identified as the top suitor and can offer an outfield-and-pitching-prospect package that fits what the Tigers need. August 3 deadline is 10 days away. Detroit is 6.5 games back in the AL Central and 4.5 games out in the Wild Card — competitive enough that the math on keeping him is real.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Tarik Skubal said publicly yesterday: "My preference is to finish the season with the Tigers and win the World Series."

ESPN's Passan still puts trade odds at 60%.

The deadline is 10 days away. The biggest name on the market just made things more complicated. #Tigers #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
The Dodgers are the top suitor for Tarik Skubal.

They can offer what the Tigers want: a controllable starter plus prospects.

Skubal wants to stay. LA wants him gone. Detroit is in the middle.

August 3. 10 days. #Tigers #Dodgers #MLB

article.headline: Skubal Says He Wants to Stay. The Dodgers Still Want Him. August 3 Is 10 Days Away.

stories.title: Red Sox Enter Buy Mode — 52-49, On a 20-3 Run, Eyeing Zach Neto
stories.angle: Boston's 15-game win streak is over, but the damage is done: they've gone from 37-48 to 52-49 and are firmly in the AL Wild Card picture on a 20-3 run in their last 23 games. They're now in buy mode ahead of the August 3 deadline. Reports indicate they have interest in Zach Neto (SS, Angels) as the team looks to add a shortstop. The Red Sox won the streak with a 1.98 team ERA and a 7-0, 1.15 ERA bullpen run.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Six weeks ago the Red Sox were 37-48.

They went on a 15-game win streak. They went 20-3 in their last 23. Now they're 52-49 and reports say they're targeting Zach Neto at shortstop.

From sellers to buyers in a month. #RedSox #MLB

article.headline: The Red Sox Went from 37-48 to Buyers. The Deadline Is in 10 Days.

stories.title: Ohtani's Timeline — One Bullpen Down, the Next One Is Saturday
stories.angle: Shohei Ohtani threw approximately 30 pitches in a bullpen session at Citizens Bank Park on July 22 — his first since receiving an Orthovisc injection in his left knee during the All-Star break. Pitching coach Mark Prior described it as a normal between-start session. Manager Dave Roberts said it will be "at least a week to 10 days" before the Dodgers even discuss a return timeline and that he "can't foresee (Ohtani) going out there and throwing 100 pitches on his first outing." The next bullpen session is expected Saturday at Citi Field in New York. Ohtani last pitched July 3.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Shohei Ohtani threw a 30-pitch bullpen session Wednesday. Pitching coach Mark Prior called it a normal side session.

Roberts: it's going to be "at least a week to 10 days" before they even discuss a return.

Next bullpen expected Saturday at Citi Field. #Dodgers #MLB

article.headline: Ohtani Threw 30 Pitches Wednesday. The Next One Is Saturday. A Return Is Still Weeks Away.

stories.title: White Sox 54-47 — The Rebuild Math Is Now Undeniable
stories.angle: White Sox lead the AL Central at 54-47, one game up on the Guardians (54-49). This is the same franchise that lost 121 games in 2024 (worst MLB record ever), 102 games in 2025, and now leads its division in July 2026 after a 30-win improvement in one off-season. Colson Montgomery has 20 home runs. Edgar Quero (22, called up from Triple-A Charlotte) replaced injured Kyle Teel. The Twins defeated Cleveland 3-1 on July 23, which helps Chicago maintain the lead.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The Chicago White Sox are 54-47 and leading the AL Central.

Two years ago they lost 121 games. The most losses in MLB history.

A 30-win improvement in one off-season. Now they're in first place in July. #WhiteSox #MLB

article.headline: The White Sox Are 54-47 and Leading the AL Central. Two Years Ago They Lost 121 Games.

stories.title: July 23 Recap — Braves 6, Padres 5: Chris Sale Strikes Out 11
stories.angle: In Thursday's Braves 6, Padres 5 win at Petco Park, Chris Sale matched his season-high with 11 strikeouts while Atlanta hit four home runs. Also notable July 23: Tigers beat Royals 4-3 on a Dillon Dingler tiebreaking double in the 8th after James Outman's 3-run HR in the 7th; Blue Jays 3, Rays 1; Twins 3, Guardians 1.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Chris Sale matched his season-high with 11 strikeouts Thursday.

The Braves also hit 4 home runs in the same game.

Atlanta won 6-5. Sale keeps building his case. #Braves #MLB

article.headline: Chris Sale Struck Out 11 Thursday. Atlanta Hit Four Home Runs. The Braves Won 6-5.
